results.question: DAU by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
DAU split by platform over the last 7 days, using {{field:active_daily.dau}} grouped by {{field:active_daily.os_platform}}.

Android is the dominant platform — running roughly 65k–75k DAU per day versus iOS at ~24k–26k, about a 2.7–2.9× gap. Both platforms trend gently upward over the window (Android peaking ~75k on Jun 21, iOS ~25.8k the same day). A small "unknown" bucket (~140–210/day) carries rows with no platform attribution. Data is available through 2026-06-22.

results.question: revenue by platform last 7 days
results.answerText: Recharge revenue for the last 7 days (Jun 17–23) came in at **~₫770.5M total**, split between just two platforms: **iOS at ₫396.0M** and **Android at ₫374.5M**. iOS edges ahead by about ₫21.4M (~51% vs 49%) — a near-even platform mix. Revenue is computed from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} broken out by {{field:user_recharge_daily.os_platform}}. Click the card to open it in the Playground and switch to a daily trend or add a country/payment-channel breakdown.

results.question: ROAS by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
Over the last 7 days (2026-06-17 → 2026-06-23), **iOS** posted the stronger ROAS at **0.59×** ({{field:game_key_metrics.rev}} ₫494M on {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}} ₫832M), while **Android** sat at **0.46×** (₫437M on ₫943M). Both are below the 1.0× break-even line, meaning ad spend hasn't been recouped within this short window — expected, since ROAS keeps maturing as installed cohorts continue to spend.

ROAS here is the native {{field:game_key_metrics.roas}} measure (revenue ÷ acquisition cost). The query also returns small `na`/`all` platform buckets (unattributed/aggregate rows with no real revenue) — I've focused the chart on the two real platforms. Open the card to slice further by {{field:game_key_metrics.media_source}}, {{field:game_key_metrics.country_code}}, or campaign.

results.question: installs by platform last 7 days
results.answerText: Over June 17–23, **Android dominated installs with 52,951 (~76%)** versus **iOS at 16,615 (~24%)**, totaling roughly 69,566 installs. The `all` and `na` platform buckets recorded zero installs in this window. Installs come from {{field:game_key_metrics.installs}} sliced by {{field:game_key_metrics.platform}}. Open the artifact to switch to a daily trend or add a country/media-source breakdown.

results.question: CPI by platform last 7 days
results.answerText:
Over the last 7 days, **{{field:game_key_metrics.cpi_vnd}}** splits sharply by platform: Android sits at ~17,919 VND per install (60,739 installs on 1.09B VND spend), while iOS runs ~52,014 VND (18,940 installs on 985M VND) — about 2.9× pricier per install. Notably, the two platforms absorbed nearly equal {{field:game_key_metrics.cost_vnd}} despite Android delivering 3.2× the install volume.

The card uses the native CPI rollup (`cost_vnd / installs`), so the ratio is computed over the full 7-day total — not an average of daily CPIs. Two extra slices appear in the table: `na` (zero spend/installs) and `all` (410M VND of cost with no attributed installs, likely unattributed/brand spend) — both excluded from the chart since their CPI is undefined.

results.question: show Minnow last 7 days
results.answerText:
Minnow-tier DAU held steady at roughly **27,000 active users/day** across the last 7 days — ranging from 26.4k (Jun 19) to 27.6k (Jun 18), with no meaningful trend. The card above charts the daily series of {{field:active_daily.dau}} filtered to {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `minnow`. Data is available through Jun 22 (today's Jun 23 snapshot isn't populated yet).

Interpreted *Minnow* as **{{field:active_daily.dau}}** (active users, 80%) since no metric was named — reply `not that` to switch to revenue, paying users, or another measure for the minnow tier.

results.question: Whale this month
results.answerText:
The artifact charts active **whale-tier** users per day for June 2026, using {{field:active_daily.dau}} filtered to {{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `whale`. Whale activity sits in a tight ~137–147/day band with no notable dips — a stable, engaged top-spender base so far this month.

One note: your message named a segment but no metric, so I defaulted to **active whales (DAU)**. If you actually meant whale **revenue/recharge**, **count of whales**, or **playtime**, tell me which and I'll swap the measure.

results.question: Whale revenue last 7 days
results.answerText: Whale-tier players ({{field:mf_users.payer_tier}} = `whale`) brought in roughly **418.7M VND** over the last 7 days. Revenue from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} sat in a tight 85–92M VND/day band from Jun 18–21, then softened to ~67.7M VND on Jun 22. The pipeline currently has data through **Jun 22** (Jun 17 and 23 returned no rows yet), so the window is effectively the five days shown.

results.question: compare Dolphin month over month
results.answerText: Pick a metric above and I'll build the month-over-month comparison for the dolphin tier.

results.question: show WAU last 7 days
results.answerText: "WAU" isn't a ready-made measure in this game — weekly active users have to be derived by rolling the distinct-user measure {{field:active_daily.dau}} up to a weekly grain (just like {{field:active_daily.mau}} gives monthly). Pick one of the options above and I'll build it.

results.question: compare WAU month over month
results.answerText: WAU can't be charted on TF, so I've offered MAU or DAU as the month-over-month metric — pick one and I'll build the comparison.

results.question: show DAU last 7 days
results.answerText: DAU sits in a stable 94k–102k band across the window, computed from {{field:active_daily.dau}} (HLL distinct active users per day). It peaked at 102,139 on June 21 and dipped to 94,299 on June 19 — no trend break. June 23 has no data yet, so the chart shows the six completed days.

results.question: revenue last 30 days
results.answerText: Total recharge revenue over the last 30 days was **≈6.16B VND**, drawn from {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} in the daily snapshot cube. The trend is clearly downward: daily revenue peaked at ~439M VND on May 26 and slid to ~134M VND by Jun 22 — a roughly 70% drop across the window, averaging ~220M VND/day. The lowest single day was Jun 11 (~120M VND). Data is available through Jun 22 (the pipeline lags one day behind today).

results.question: DAU by country last 7 days
results.answerText:
Here's DAU by country for the last 7 days (2026-06-17 → 2026-06-23), using {{field:active_daily.dau}} grouped by {{field:active_daily.country_code}}.

Three SEA markets dominate: **Indonesia ~73.9K**, **Thailand ~64.2K**, and **Vietnam ~42.6K**. Everything else is an order of magnitude smaller — Malaysia ~5.1K, then Singapore, Cambodia, Laos, Philippines, and Japan all under ~1K each. A small ~816-user slice has no country code attributed (shown as "Unknown"). The bar chart and full table are in the attached card.

results.question: top 10 players by revenue
results.answerText:
Here are the **top 10 players by revenue** over the last 30 days, ranked by {{field:user_recharge_daily.revenue_vnd_total}} with their {{field:user_recharge_daily.txn_count_total}} alongside.

The leaderboard is steeply concentrated: the single top spender recharged **₫302.9M** across 158 transactions, followed by ₫253.1M and ₫188.3M. From rank 4 down it tapers from ~₫112M to ~₫63M. Worth noting transaction count doesn't track revenue rank — e.g. the rank-9 player spent ₫64M across only 10 transactions (large per-purchase tickets), while some lower-revenue players made 100+ smaller buys.

I used the {{field:user_recharge_daily.user_id}} grain on the pre-aggregated daily snapshot cube and defaulted to a 30-day window since you didn't specify one — click the card to switch to 7-day, lifetime, or a custom range.

results.question: asdf qwer
results.answerText:
That doesn't look like an analytics question — it reads like a keyboard test. 🙂

I'm the Cube Playground assistant for **tf**. I turn natural-language questions about your game's data into clickable, ready-to-run query cards.

Try something like:
- "Show me daily revenue for the last 30 days"
- "What's DAU this week?"
- "Top spenders this month"
- "Churn rate trend"

Or just ask **"what can I explore in this game?"** and I'll orient you. What would you like to look at?
